description: observation_period defines enrollment windows and has no clinical *_concept_id column (only period_type_concept_id for provenance). Filtering observation_period with clinical concept IDs (condition, drug, etc.) is a table confusion error.
logic: If observation_period is filtered using concept IDs that belong to clinical domains (Condition, Drug, Procedure, Measurement) rather than Type Concept domain, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation_period WHERE period_type_concept_id = 201826 -- This is a condition concept
example_correct: SELECT * FROM observation_period WHERE period_type_concept_id = 44814724 -- Period while enrolled in study

name: or_condition_on_different_concept_id_columns
description: Using OR between different *_concept_id columns in a single WHERE clause (e.g., condition_concept_id = X OR drug_concept_id = Y) is always wrong because a single table row cannot have both columns. This indicates confusion between tables.
logic: If a WHERE clause contains OR between *_concept_id columns from different domain tables within the same table reference, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826 OR drug_concept_id = 1125315
example_correct: SELECT person_id FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826 UNION SELECT person_id FROM drug_exposure WHERE drug_concept_id = 1125315

name: device_exposure_no_days_supply
description: device_exposure does not have a days_supply column (that column belongs to drug_exposure). Device duration is computed from device_exposure_start_date and device_exposure_end_date.
logic: If a query references device_exposure.days_supply, flag as error — this column does not exist on device_exposure.
example_violation: SELECT days_supply FROM device_exposure WHERE device_concept_id = 4222658
example_correct: SELECT DATEDIFF(day, device_exposure_start_date, device_exposure_end_date) AS duration FROM device_exposure WHERE device_concept_id = 4222658

name: device_exposure_no_refills
description: device_exposure does not have a refills column (that column belongs to drug_exposure). Referencing refills on device_exposure is a column error.
logic: If a query references device_exposure.refills, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT refills FROM device_exposure WHERE device_concept_id = 4222658
example_correct: SELECT quantity FROM device_exposure WHERE device_concept_id = 4222658

name: visit_detail_preceding_id_self_join
description: visit_detail.preceding_visit_detail_id references another visit_detail_id in the same table (temporal chaining). This self-join must target visit_detail on visit_detail_id, not visit_occurrence.
logic: If preceding_visit_detail_id is joined to any table other than visit_detail, or to any column other than visit_detail_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail vd JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON vd.preceding_visit_detail_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id
example_correct: SELECT curr.*, prev.visit_detail_start_date AS prev_start FROM visit_detail curr JOIN visit_detail prev ON curr.preceding_visit_detail_id = prev.visit_detail_id

name: concept_synonym_language_concept_id
description: concept_synonym.language_concept_id specifies the language of the synonym. For English-language queries, filter language_concept_id = 4180186 (English). Without this, results may include synonyms in other languages.
logic: If concept_synonym is queried with LIKE on concept_synonym_name but without filtering language_concept_id, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT concept_id FROM concept_synonym WHERE concept_synonym_name LIKE '%heart attack%'
example_correct: SELECT concept_id FROM concept_synonym WHERE concept_synonym_name LIKE '%heart attack%' AND language_concept_id = 4180186

name: vocabulary_table_join_uses_vocabulary_id_string
description: Joins from concept to vocabulary must use concept.vocabulary_id = vocabulary.vocabulary_id (both VARCHAR). Using vocabulary.vocabulary_concept_id for this join is incorrect — vocabulary_concept_id is the concept that represents the vocabulary itself.
logic: If concept is joined to vocabulary on concept.vocabulary_id = vocabulary.vocabulary_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN vocabulary v ON c.vocabulary_id = v.vocabulary_concept_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN vocabulary v ON c.vocabulary_id = v.vocabulary_id

name: domain_table_join_uses_domain_id_string
description: Joins from concept to domain must use concept.domain_id = domain.domain_id (both VARCHAR). Using domain.domain_concept_id for this join is incorrect.
logic: If concept is joined to domain on concept.domain_id = domain.domain_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN domain d ON c.domain_id = d.domain_concept_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN domain d ON c.domain_id = d.domain_id

name: concept_class_table_join_uses_concept_class_id_string
description: Joins from concept to concept_class must use concept.concept_class_id = concept_class.concept_class_id (both VARCHAR). Using concept_class.concept_class_concept_id for this join is incorrect.
logic: If concept is joined to concept_class on concept.concept_class_id = concept_class.concept_class_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN concept_class cc ON c.concept_class_id = cc.concept_class_concept_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN concept_class cc ON c.concept_class_id = cc.concept_class_id

name: having_without_group_by
description: HAVING clauses without GROUP BY are syntactically valid in some SQL dialects but almost always indicate a query logic error in OMOP analytical queries.
logic: If a query contains a HAVING clause but no GROUP BY clause, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
example_correct: SELECT condition_concept_id, COUNT(*) FROM condition_occurrence GROUP BY condition_concept_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 5

name: age_at_event_not_age_at_query_time
description: Epidemiological studies require age at the time of the clinical event, not age at query execution time. Compute age using the event date minus year_of_birth, not GETDATE()/CURRENT_DATE minus year_of_birth.
logic: If a query computes age using GETDATE(), CURRENT_DATE, NOW(), or SYSDATE minus year_of_birth/birth_datetime, and the query context is analyzing historical clinical events, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT co.person_id, YEAR(GETDATE()) - p.year_of_birth AS age FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN person p ON co.person_id = p.person_id WHERE co.condition_concept_id = 201826
example_correct: SELECT co.person_id, YEAR(co.condition_start_date) - p.year_of_birth AS age_at_diagnosis FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN person p ON co.person_id = p.person_id WHERE co.condition_concept_id = 201826

name: classification_concept_not_in_clinical_table
description: Classification concepts (standard_concept = 'C') are used for hierarchy navigation only and should not appear in clinical table *_concept_id columns. Only standard concepts (standard_concept = 'S') should be used for clinical filtering.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id used in a clinical table's primary *_concept_id filter has standard_concept = 'C' (Classification) instead of 'S' (Standard), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 441840 -- Clinical Finding (Classification concept, not assignable)
example_correct: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id IN (SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 441840)

name: death_join_to_person_not_to_clinical_event
description: The death table joins to person on person_id. It does not have visit_occurrence_id or condition_occurrence_id. To associate death with a clinical event, join both tables to person independently.
logic: If death is joined directly to condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, or visit_occurrence on any column other than person_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death d JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON d.person_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM death d JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON d.person_id = vo.person_id

name: cost_payer_plan_period_id_join
description: cost.payer_plan_period_id is a FK to payer_plan_period.payer_plan_period_id. Joining cost to payer_plan_period on person_id or cost_event_id is incorrect.
logic: If cost is joined to payer_plan_period on any column other than payer_plan_period_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM cost c JOIN payer_plan_period pp ON c.cost_event_id = pp.payer_plan_period_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM cost c JOIN payer_plan_period pp ON c.payer_plan_period_id = pp.payer_plan_period_id

name: cost_drg_concept_id_domain
description: cost.drg_concept_id should reference concepts from the DRG domain (Diagnosis Related Groups). Using condition or procedure concepts in this column is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in cost.drg_concept_id resolves to a concept with domain_id != 'DRG' in the concept table, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM cost WHERE drg_concept_id = 201826 -- This is a Condition concept, not DRG
example_correct: SELECT * FROM cost WHERE drg_concept_id = 38000280 -- Actual DRG concept

name: cost_revenue_code_concept_id_domain
description: cost.revenue_code_concept_id should reference concepts from the Revenue Code domain. Using concepts from other domains is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in cost.revenue_code_concept_id resolves to a concept with domain_id != 'Revenue Code', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM cost WHERE revenue_code_concept_id = 1125315 -- Drug concept, not Revenue Code
example_correct: SELECT * FROM cost WHERE revenue_code_concept_id = 38003190 -- Revenue Code concept

name: episode_parent_id_self_join
description: episode.episode_parent_id references another episode.episode_id in the same table (hierarchical episode nesting). This self-join must target the episode table on episode_id, not another table.
logic: If episode_parent_id is joined to any table other than episode, or to any column other than episode_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM episode e JOIN condition_occurrence co ON e.episode_parent_id = co.condition_occurrence_id
example_correct: SELECT child.*, parent.episode_concept_id AS parent_type FROM episode child JOIN episode parent ON child.episode_parent_id = parent.episode_id

name: episode_event_no_person_id
description: episode_event has no person_id column. To get patient-level data from episode events, join episode_event to episode on episode_id, then episode to person on person_id.
logic: If episode_event is joined directly to person or filtered by person_id, flag as error — episode_event has no person_id.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM episode_event ee JOIN person p ON ee.person_id = p.person_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM episode_event ee JOIN episode e ON ee.episode_id = e.episode_id JOIN person p ON e.person_id = p.person_id

name: subquery_in_where_without_correlation
description: Uncorrelated subqueries in WHERE IN clauses on large vocabulary tables (e.g., SELECT concept_id FROM concept WHERE ...) execute once and are efficient. But uncorrelated subqueries returning millions of rows from clinical tables can cause severe performance issues.
logic: If a WHERE IN contains an uncorrelated subquery against a large clinical table without a restrictive WHERE clause (returning potentially millions of IDs), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE person_id IN (SELECT person_id FROM measurement)
example_correct: SELECT * FROM person WHERE person_id IN (SELECT person_id FROM measurement WHERE measurement_concept_id = 3004249 AND value_as_number > 6.5)

name: observation_value_as_columns_mutually_contextual
description: In the observation table, value_as_number, value_as_string, and value_as_concept_id represent the same result in different formats. A query should use the appropriate column based on the observation_concept_id's expected data type, not combine them with AND.
logic: If a WHERE clause applies AND conditions on two or more of value_as_number, value_as_string, and value_as_concept_id for the same observation row, flag as warning — typically only one is populated per row.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE value_as_number > 100 AND value_as_string = 'High'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE observation_concept_id = 4058286 AND value_as_concept_id = 45884084

name: visit_detail_has_no_preceding_visit_occurrence_id
description: visit_detail does not have a preceding_visit_occurrence_id column. The temporal chain in visit_detail uses preceding_visit_detail_id. Confusing these two columns across tables is an error.
logic: If a query references visit_detail.preceding_visit_occurrence_id, flag as error — that column exists only on visit_occurrence.
example_violation: SELECT preceding_visit_occurrence_id FROM visit_detail
example_correct: SELECT preceding_visit_detail_id FROM visit_detail

name: measurement_time_is_varchar
description: measurement.measurement_time is a VARCHAR field storing time as a string (e.g., '14:30:00'), not a TIME or DATETIME type. Using temporal arithmetic or DATEDIFF directly on it without CAST is incorrect.
logic: If measurement_time is used in DATEDIFF, DATEADD, or direct comparison with a TIME/DATETIME literal without explicit CAST/CONVERT, flag as warning. Recommend using measurement_datetime instead.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_time > '14:00:00'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_datetime >= '2023-01-01 14:00:00'

name: payer_plan_period_sponsor_concept_id_domain
description: payer_plan_period.sponsor_concept_id identifies the sponsor of the health plan and should use concepts from the Sponsor domain. Using clinical concepts (Condition, Drug) is incorrect.
logic: If hardcoded concept_ids in sponsor_concept_id, payer_concept_id, or plan_concept_id resolve to clinical domain concepts rather than Payer/Sponsor/Plan domains, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM payer_plan_period WHERE payer_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept used as payer
example_correct: SELECT * FROM payer_plan_period WHERE payer_concept_id = 38000280 -- Actual payer concept

name: location_history_entity_id_requires_domain_id
description: location_history.entity_id is a polymorphic FK whose target is identified by location_history.domain_id. Without filtering domain_id, entity_id may match IDs from any table (person, provider, care_site).
logic: If location_history.entity_id is joined to a table's primary key without also filtering location_history.domain_id to the appropriate value, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM location_history lh JOIN person p ON lh.entity_id = p.person_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM location_history lh JOIN person p ON lh.entity_id = p.person_id WHERE lh.domain_id = 'Person'

name: procedure_date_not_procedure_start_date
description: procedure_occurrence uses procedure_date (not procedure_start_date). Unlike condition_occurrence which has condition_start_date, the column name for procedures omits 'start'. Referencing procedure_start_date is a column name error.
logic: If a query references procedure_occurrence.procedure_start_date, flag as error — the correct column is procedure_date.
example_violation: SELECT procedure_start_date FROM procedure_occurrence WHERE procedure_concept_id = 2213473
example_correct: SELECT procedure_date FROM procedure_occurrence WHERE procedure_concept_id = 2213473

name: concept_ancestor_not_for_source_to_standard_mapping
description: concept_ancestor provides hierarchical relationships (parent-child) among standard concepts. It is not for source-to-standard mapping. Use concept_relationship with relationship_id = 'Maps to' for source-to-standard mapping instead.
logic: If concept_ancestor is used in a context where a non-standard source concept is placed in ancestor_concept_id or descendant_concept_id to find its standard equivalent, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 44836914 -- ICD10CM source concept; concept_ancestor won't have it
example_correct: SELECT concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship WHERE concept_id_1 = 44836914 AND relationship_id = 'Maps to' AND invalid_reason IS NULL

name: observation_period_not_for_clinical_event_lookup
description: observation_period tracks patient enrollment/data availability windows. It is not a clinical event table. Querying it to find conditions, drugs, or procedures is a table confusion error.
logic: If observation_period is filtered with clinical concept IDs or joined as if it were condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, etc., flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation_period WHERE observation_period_id IN (SELECT condition_occurrence_id FROM condition_occurrence)
example_correct: SELECT * FROM observation_period op WHERE op.person_id = 12345

name: left_join_then_where_on_right_table
description: A LEFT JOIN followed by a WHERE clause filtering on a column of the right table (other than IS NULL) effectively converts the LEFT JOIN to an INNER JOIN, silently dropping the unmatched rows the LEFT JOIN was meant to preserve.
logic: If a LEFT JOIN is followed by a WHERE clause filtering a column from the right-side table with any condition other than IS NULL or IS NOT NULL, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT co.* FROM condition_occurrence co LEFT JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON co.visit_occurrence_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id WHERE vo.visit_concept_id = 9201
example_correct: SELECT co.* FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON co.visit_occurrence_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id WHERE vo.visit_concept_id = 9201

name: relationship_table_is_hierarchical_defines_ancestry_flags
description: The relationship table has is_hierarchical and defines_ancestry columns. When programmatically discovering hierarchical relationships, use these flags rather than hardcoding relationship_id values.
logic: If a query filters relationship.is_hierarchical or relationship.defines_ancestry with non-boolean values (e.g., string 'true', integer 2), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM relationship WHERE is_hierarchical = 'true'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM relationship WHERE is_hierarchical = 1

name: death_date_before_event_date_illogical
description: If a query correlates death with clinical events, the death_date should logically be on or after the clinical event date. A filter where death_date < event start date may indicate reversed logic.
logic: If a query joins death to a clinical event table and includes a condition where death_date < the event's start date (implying events after death), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death d JOIN condition_occurrence co ON d.person_id = co.person_id WHERE co.condition_start_date > d.death_date + INTERVAL '30' DAY
example_correct: SELECT * FROM death d JOIN condition_occurrence co ON d.person_id = co.person_id WHERE co.condition_start_date <= d.death_date

name: note_nlp_snippet_not_structured_data
description: note_nlp.snippet contains a short text excerpt around the NLP-extracted term. It is free text for context, not structured data. Do not use it in JOINs, numeric comparisons, or as a concept identifier.
logic: If note_nlp.snippet or note_nlp.lexical_variant is used in a JOIN to concept or in an equality filter for clinical identification instead of note_nlp_concept_id, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE snippet = 'diabetes'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE note_nlp_concept_id = 201826 AND term_exists = 'Y'

name: specimen_disease_status_concept_id_domain
description: specimen.disease_status_concept_id encodes the disease status of the specimen (e.g., malignant, benign) and should use concepts from the appropriate domain. Using general condition or drug concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in specimen.disease_status_concept_id resolves to a concept whose domain_id is 'Condition', 'Drug', or 'Procedure' rather than a disease status qualifier, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM specimen WHERE disease_status_concept_id = 201826 -- General condition concept misused
example_correct: SELECT * FROM specimen WHERE disease_status_concept_id = 4188540 -- Malignant neoplasm status

name: source_to_concept_map_target_concept_should_be_standard
description: source_to_concept_map.target_concept_id should resolve to a standard concept (standard_concept = 'S'). If the target is non-standard, the mapping is likely stale or incorrectly populated.
logic: If a query uses source_to_concept_map.target_concept_id without verifying via concept table that the target has standard_concept = 'S', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT target_concept_id FROM source_to_concept_map WHERE source_code = '250.00' AND source_vocabulary_id = 'ICD9CM' AND invalid_reason IS NULL
example_correct: SELECT stcm.target_concept_id FROM source_to_concept_map stcm JOIN concept c ON stcm.target_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE stcm.source_code = '250.00' AND stcm.source_vocabulary_id = 'ICD9CM' AND stcm.invalid_reason IS NULL AND c.standard_concept = 'S'

name: condition_occurrence_no_value_as_number
description: condition_occurrence does not have value_as_number, value_as_concept_id, or unit_concept_id columns. These belong to measurement and observation. Referencing them on condition_occurrence is a column error.
logic: If a query references condition_occurrence.value_as_number, condition_occurrence.value_as_concept_id, or condition_occurrence.unit_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT value_as_number FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826
example_correct: SELECT value_as_number FROM measurement WHERE measurement_concept_id = 3004249

name: procedure_occurrence_no_value_as_number
description: procedure_occurrence does not have value_as_number or value_as_concept_id columns. Procedure results are not stored here. If a procedure produces a result, it is typically stored in measurement.
logic: If a query references procedure_occurrence.value_as_number or procedure_occurrence.value_as_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT value_as_number FROM procedure_occurrence WHERE procedure_concept_id = 2213473
example_correct: SELECT m.value_as_number FROM measurement m WHERE m.measurement_concept_id = 3004249

name: concept_id_used_as_string_comparison
description: All *_concept_id columns are INTEGER type. Comparing them with string literals (e.g., concept_id = '201826') relies on implicit casting and may fail or behave unexpectedly depending on the database engine.
logic: If a *_concept_id column is compared with a quoted string literal (e.g., = '201826'), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = '201826'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826

name: note_class_concept_id_for_note_category
description: To identify the clinical category of a note (progress note, discharge summary, radiology report), use note.note_class_concept_id, not note.note_type_concept_id. The type concept indicates provenance (EHR, claim), not the clinical note category.
logic: If note.note_type_concept_id is filtered with concept_ids representing clinical note categories (e.g., Discharge Summary, Progress Note) rather than provenance types, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note WHERE note_type_concept_id = 44814637 -- Discharge Summary is a note class, not a type
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note WHERE note_class_concept_id = 44814637 -- Discharge Summary as note class

name: drug_exposure_end_date_null_handling
description: drug_exposure.drug_exposure_end_date can be NULL when the end of exposure is unknown. Queries computing treatment overlaps, concurrent medications, or durations must handle NULLs to avoid silently dropping records or producing NULL results.
logic: If a query computes DATEDIFF or temporal overlap on drug_exposure_end_date without COALESCE, ISNULL, or an IS NOT NULL filter, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure d1 JOIN drug_exposure d2 ON d1.person_id = d2.person_id WHERE d1.drug_exposure_end_date > d2.drug_exposure_start_date
example_correct: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure d1 JOIN drug_exposure d2 ON d1.person_id = d2.person_id WHERE COALESCE(d1.drug_exposure_end_date, DATEADD(day, d1.days_supply, d1.drug_exposure_start_date)) > d2.drug_exposure_start_date

name: visit_occurrence_no_condition_concept_id
description: visit_occurrence stores visit metadata (type, dates, location) and does not contain clinical event concept columns like condition_concept_id, drug_concept_id, or measurement_concept_id. Clinical events are in their respective tables linked via visit_occurrence_id.
logic: If a query references visit_occurrence.condition_concept_id, visit_occurrence.drug_concept_id, or visit_occurrence.measurement_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT condition_concept_id FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_occurrence_id = 12345
example_correct: SELECT co.condition_concept_id FROM condition_occurrence co WHERE co.visit_occurrence_id = 12345

name: concept_name_like_without_domain_constraint
description: Searching concept.concept_name with LIKE without also filtering domain_id returns matches across all domains (Condition, Drug, Procedure, etc.). This produces irrelevant results — e.g., searching '%infection%' returns conditions, procedures, and measurements alike.
logic: If concept.concept_name is filtered with LIKE/ILIKE and neither domain_id nor vocabulary_id is constrained in the same WHERE scope, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE concept_name LIKE '%infection%' AND standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_ancestor_descendant_swapped_for_rollup
description: When rolling up clinical events to a parent concept (e.g., counting all diabetes subtypes under a parent), the clinical table's *_concept_id should join to descendant_concept_id, and the parent concept should be in ancestor_concept_id. Swapping these reverses the hierarchy.
logic: If a clinical table's *_concept_id is joined to concept_ancestor.ancestor_concept_id and the WHERE clause filters on descendant_concept_id, flag as error — the join direction is inverted for rollup queries.
example_violation: SELECT ca.descendant_concept_id, COUNT(*) FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept_ancestor ca ON co.condition_concept_id = ca.ancestor_concept_id WHERE ca.descendant_concept_id = 201820 GROUP BY ca.descendant_concept_id

name: concept_relationship_maps_to_target_must_be_standard
description: When using concept_relationship with relationship_id = 'Maps to', concept_id_2 (the target) must be a standard concept. If the query does not verify standard_concept = 'S' on the target concept, it may return non-standard or deprecated mappings.
logic: If concept_relationship is used with relationship_id = 'Maps to' and the target concept_id_2 is not validated against concept.standard_concept = 'S' via a join, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT cr.concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship cr WHERE cr.concept_id_1 = 44836914 AND cr.relationship_id = 'Maps to' AND cr.invalid_reason IS NULL

name: vocabulary_id_case_sensitive_mismatch
description: vocabulary_id values are case-sensitive strings with canonical casing (e.g., 'SNOMED', 'RxNorm', 'ICD10CM', 'ICD9CM', 'LOINC', 'CPT4', 'HCPCS', 'NDC', 'ATC'). Common misspellings include 'Snomed', 'RXNORM', 'ICD-10', 'icd10cm', 'Loinc'.
logic: If concept.vocabulary_id is filtered with a value that does not match known canonical vocabulary_id casing (e.g., 'RXNORM' instead of 'RxNorm', 'Snomed' instead of 'SNOMED'), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE vocabulary_id = 'RXNORM' AND concept_name LIKE '%aspirin%'

name: vocabulary_id_with_hyphens_or_spaces
description: OMOP vocabulary_id values never contain hyphens or spaces. Common errors include 'ICD-10-CM', 'ICD-9', 'RxNorm Extension', 'CPT-4'. The correct values are 'ICD10CM', 'ICD9CM', 'RxNorm Extension' (note: this one does have a space), 'CPT4'.
logic: If vocabulary_id contains hyphens (e.g., 'ICD-10-CM', 'ICD-9-CM', 'CPT-4'), flag as error. These are malformed vocabulary identifiers.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE vocabulary_id = 'ICD-10-CM' AND concept_code = 'E11.9'

name: domain_id_case_sensitive_mismatch
description: domain_id values are case-sensitive with specific casing: 'Condition', 'Drug', 'Procedure', 'Measurement', 'Observation', 'Device', 'Spec Anatomic Site', 'Meas Value', 'Route', 'Unit', 'Visit', 'Type Concept', 'Race', 'Ethnicity', 'Gender'. Common errors include 'condition', 'DRUG', 'procedure'.
logic: If concept.domain_id is filtered with a value not matching known canonical casing (e.g., 'condition' instead of 'Condition', 'DRUG' instead of 'Drug'), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE domain_id = 'condition' AND standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_class_id_case_sensitive_mismatch
description: concept_class_id values are case-sensitive. Common values include 'Clinical Finding', 'Procedure', 'Ingredient', 'Clinical Drug', 'Branded Drug', '4-dig billing code', 'Lab Test'. Common errors: 'clinical finding', 'INGREDIENT', 'Clinical drug'.
logic: If concept_class_id is filtered with a value not matching known canonical casing, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE concept_class_id = 'clinical finding' AND standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_ancestor_max_levels_misused_as_distance
description: concept_ancestor.max_levels_of_separation represents the longest path in the hierarchy, not a unique distance metric. Filtering max_levels_of_separation = 1 does NOT reliably return only direct children — use min_levels_of_separation = 1 for immediate parent-child relationships.
logic: If a query filters concept_ancestor.max_levels_of_separation = 1 intending to find direct children, flag as warning and suggest min_levels_of_separation = 1 instead.
example_violation: SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 201820 AND max_levels_of_separation = 1

name: standard_concept_is_null_for_non_standard_lookup
description: Non-standard concepts have standard_concept IS NULL (not standard_concept = 'N' or standard_concept = ''). Filtering standard_concept = NULL (without IS) or standard_concept = '' returns zero rows.
logic: If a query filters concept.standard_concept = NULL (equality, not IS NULL) or standard_concept = '' or standard_concept = 'N', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE concept_code = 'E11.9' AND vocabulary_id = 'ICD10CM' AND standard_concept = NULL

name: concept_relationship_subsumes_vs_is_a_direction
description: 'Is a' goes from child (concept_id_1) to parent (concept_id_2). 'Subsumes' is the reverse — from parent (concept_id_1) to child (concept_id_2). Using the wrong relationship_id for the desired direction produces incorrect hierarchical results.
logic: If a query uses relationship_id = 'Is a' to find children of a concept (filtering the parent in concept_id_1), flag as error — 'Is a' finds parents, not children. Use 'Subsumes' or swap direction.
example_violation: SELECT cr.concept_id_2 AS child FROM concept_relationship cr WHERE cr.concept_id_1 = 201820 AND cr.relationship_id = 'Is a'

name: concept_relationship_mapped_from_vs_maps_to
description: 'Maps to' goes from source (concept_id_1) to standard (concept_id_2). 'Mapped from' is the reverse. To find the standard concept for a source concept, use 'Maps to' with the source in concept_id_1. To find all source concepts for a standard concept, use 'Mapped from' with the standard in concept_id_1.
logic: If a query uses relationship_id = 'Maps to' with a known standard concept in concept_id_1, flag as warning — standard concepts are already standard; use 'Mapped from' to discover source concepts that map to it.
example_violation: SELECT cr.concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship cr WHERE cr.concept_id_1 = 201826 AND cr.relationship_id = 'Maps to' -- 201826 is already standard SNOMED

name: concept_code_like_wildcard_without_vocabulary_id
description: Using LIKE on concept.concept_code without specifying vocabulary_id is dangerous because concept codes have different formats across vocabularies. A pattern like '%250%' matches ICD codes, SNOMED codes, NDC codes, and LOINC codes simultaneously.
logic: If concept.concept_code is filtered with LIKE and vocabulary_id is not constrained, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept WHERE concept_code LIKE '250%'

name: multiple_maps_to_targets_not_handled
description: A single source concept can map to multiple standard concepts via 'Maps to'. Queries that assume a 1:1 mapping (e.g., using = instead of IN, or not using DISTINCT) may silently duplicate clinical records or miss mappings.
logic: If concept_relationship with relationship_id = 'Maps to' is used in a scalar subquery (SELECT single value) or joined without DISTINCT/aggregation awareness, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT (SELECT cr.concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship cr WHERE cr.concept_id_1 = c.concept_id AND cr.relationship_id = 'Maps to' AND cr.invalid_reason IS NULL) AS standard_id FROM concept c WHERE c.concept_code = 'E11.9'

name: concept_ancestor_used_across_domains
description: concept_ancestor hierarchies are domain-specific. An ancestor_concept_id from the Condition domain will only have Condition descendants. Queries should not expect cross-domain results (e.g., a Drug descendant of a Condition ancestor).
logic: If a query uses concept_ancestor and then filters the descendant concepts by a domain_id different from the ancestor's domain_id, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT ca.descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor ca JOIN concept c ON ca.descendant_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE ca.ancestor_concept_id = 201820 AND c.domain_id = 'Drug'

name: concept_relationship_no_join_to_concept_on_both_sides
description: When resolving concept_relationship, both concept_id_1 and concept_id_2 typically need to be joined to the concept table (with separate aliases) to access concept_name, vocabulary_id, or domain_id. Joining only one side leaves the other unresolved.
logic: If concept_relationship is used in a query that joins concept only on concept_id_1 but references attributes of concept_id_2 (or vice versa) without a second join, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c1.concept_name, cr.concept_id_2 FROM concept c1 JOIN concept_relationship cr ON c1.concept_id = cr.concept_id_1 WHERE cr.relationship_id = 'Maps to'

name: standard_concept_filter_with_or_instead_of_in
description: Filtering standard_concept with OR logic (standard_concept = 'S' OR standard_concept = 'C') when only standard concepts are desired is incorrect. Classification concepts ('C') should not be used in clinical table lookups. Use = 'S' alone for standard concept filtering.
logic: If a query filters concept for use in a clinical table's primary *_concept_id and includes standard_concept = 'C' in an OR condition with 'S', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT concept_id FROM concept WHERE concept_name LIKE '%diabetes%' AND (standard_concept = 'S' OR standard_concept = 'C')

name: concept_ancestor_with_min_zero_unintentional_self_include
description: concept_ancestor always includes a self-referencing row (ancestor = descendant, min_levels_of_separation = 0). When building concept sets via concept_ancestor, this is typically desired (include the anchor concept itself). But queries that separately add the anchor AND use concept_ancestor will double-count it.
logic: If a query uses concept_ancestor with a WHERE ancestor_concept_id = X AND also separately includes concept_id = X via OR or UNION, flag as warning — the ancestor is already included in concept_ancestor results.
example_violation: SELECT concept_id FROM concept WHERE concept_id = 201820 UNION SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 201820

name: concept_relationship_multiple_relationship_types_without_filter
description: A single pair of concepts can have multiple relationship types in concept_relationship (e.g., 'Maps to', 'Is a', 'Has finding site'). Joining concept_relationship without filtering relationship_id multiplies rows by the number of relationship types.
logic: If concept_relationship is in a JOIN and no WHERE or ON condition restricts relationship_id, and the query is not explicitly enumerating all relationships, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c2.concept_name FROM concept c1 JOIN concept_relationship cr ON c1.concept_id = cr.concept_id_1 JOIN concept c2 ON cr.concept_id_2 = c2.concept_id WHERE c1.concept_id = 201826

name: standard_concept_filter_missing_on_concept_name_join
description: When joining a clinical table to concept to resolve concept_name, the query should still filter standard_concept = 'S' if it also uses the result for further filtering. Without this, the join may resolve to non-standard concept names that don't match the standardized data.
logic: If concept is joined to a clinical table on a primary *_concept_id and the query then filters on concept.concept_name, concept.vocabulary_id, or concept.concept_class_id without also including standard_concept = 'S', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.vocabulary_id = 'ICD10CM'

name: source_concept_id_should_join_concept_without_standard_filter
description: When resolving *_source_concept_id columns (e.g., condition_source_concept_id), the join to concept should NOT filter standard_concept = 'S'. Source concepts are intentionally non-standard.
logic: If a *_source_concept_id column is joined to concept and the WHERE clause includes standard_concept = 'S', flag as error — source concepts are non-standard by definition.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_source_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_id_zero_not_joined_to_concept
description: concept_id = 0 is a special 'No matching concept' placeholder. Joining it to the concept table to resolve concept_name returns 'No matching concept' — which is technically valid but often indicates the query should have excluded zero-concept records first.
logic: If a query joins a clinical *_concept_id to concept without excluding concept_id = 0 and then groups or filters by concept_name, flag as warning — the 'No matching concept' bucket may distort results.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name, COUNT(*) FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_concept_id = c.concept_id GROUP BY c.concept_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC

name: snomed_vocabulary_id_for_conditions
description: Standard condition concepts in OMOP use vocabulary_id = 'SNOMED'. Filtering condition_concept_id lookups with vocabulary_id = 'ICD10CM' or 'ICD9CM' returns source (non-standard) concepts, not the standard concepts stored in condition_occurrence.condition_concept_id.
logic: If concept is joined to condition_occurrence.condition_concept_id and filtered with vocabulary_id IN ('ICD10CM', 'ICD9CM', 'ICD10', 'ICD9'), flag as error — standard condition concepts are SNOMED.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.vocabulary_id = 'ICD10CM'

name: rxnorm_vocabulary_id_for_drugs
description: Standard drug concepts use vocabulary_id = 'RxNorm' (or 'RxNorm Extension'). Filtering drug_concept_id lookups with vocabulary_id = 'NDC' or 'ATC' returns source concepts, not the standard concepts in drug_exposure.drug_concept_id.
logic: If concept is joined to drug_exposure.drug_concept_id and filtered with vocabulary_id IN ('NDC', 'ATC', 'GCN_SEQNO', 'SPL'), flag as error — standard drug concepts are RxNorm.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure de JOIN concept c ON de.drug_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.vocabulary_id = 'NDC'

name: snomed_vocabulary_id_for_procedures_or_cpt4
description: Standard procedure concepts use vocabulary_id = 'SNOMED' or 'CPT4' or 'HCPCS'. Filtering procedure_concept_id with source vocabularies like 'ICD10PCS' or 'ICD9Proc' returns non-standard concepts.
logic: If concept is joined to procedure_occurrence.procedure_concept_id and filtered with vocabulary_id IN ('ICD10PCS', 'ICD9Proc', 'OPCS4'), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM procedure_occurrence po JOIN concept c ON po.procedure_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.vocabulary_id = 'ICD10PCS'

name: loinc_vocabulary_id_for_measurements
description: Standard measurement concepts primarily use vocabulary_id = 'LOINC'. Filtering measurement_concept_id with other vocabularies (e.g., 'SNOMED', 'CPT4') returns concepts that may not be standard measurement concepts.
logic: If concept is joined to measurement.measurement_concept_id and filtered with vocabulary_id NOT IN ('LOINC', 'SNOMED') while also requiring standard_concept = 'S', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement m JOIN concept c ON m.measurement_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.vocabulary_id = 'CPT4' AND c.standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_ancestor_with_concept_class_filter_on_descendant
description: When using concept_ancestor to find descendants and then filtering by concept_class_id, ensure the filter is applied to the descendant concept, not the ancestor. Filtering the ancestor by concept_class_id constrains the starting point, not the results.
logic: If concept_ancestor is joined to concept on ancestor_concept_id and the query filters concept.concept_class_id intending to restrict descendant results, flag as warning — the filter is on the wrong side.
example_violation: SELECT ca.descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor ca JOIN concept c ON ca.ancestor_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE ca.ancestor_concept_id = 21600001 AND c.concept_class_id = 'Ingredient'

name: concept_relationship_concept_id_1_equals_concept_id_2
description: A concept_relationship row where concept_id_1 = concept_id_2 is a self-referencing relationship. Queries building concept sets via 'Maps to' should be aware that a standard concept maps to itself, which may cause duplication if not handled.
logic: If a query uses concept_relationship with 'Maps to' and does not account for possible self-mapping (concept_id_1 = concept_id_2), and the result feeds into a JOIN that could duplicate rows, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT cr.concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship cr WHERE cr.relationship_id = 'Maps to' AND cr.concept_id_1 IN (201826, 44836914) AND cr.invalid_reason IS NULL -- 201826 maps to itself

name: unit_concept_id_vocabulary_is_ucum
description: Standard unit concepts use vocabulary_id = 'UCUM'. Filtering unit_concept_id lookups with other vocabulary IDs returns non-standard units.
logic: If concept is joined to a unit_concept_id column and filtered with vocabulary_id != 'UCUM', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement m JOIN concept c ON m.unit_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.vocabulary_id = 'SNOMED'

name: concept_ancestor_depth_filter_off_by_one
description: min_levels_of_separation = 0 is the concept itself, = 1 is direct children, = 2 is grandchildren, etc. Queries that filter min_levels_of_separation = 1 intending 'the concept and its direct children' miss the concept itself (which is at level 0).
logic: If concept_ancestor is filtered with min_levels_of_separation = 1 and the query context suggests both the anchor and children are needed, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 201820 AND min_levels_of_separation = 1 -- Misses 201820 itself

name: concept_code_equality_across_vocabularies
description: The same concept_code value can exist in multiple vocabularies with completely different meanings. Filtering concept_code = 'X' without vocabulary_id and then using the concept_id in a clinical table is dangerous.
logic: If a subquery or CTE selects concept_id from concept filtered by concept_code without vocabulary_id, and that result is used to filter a clinical table, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id IN (SELECT concept_id FROM concept WHERE concept_code = '38341003')

name: concept_valid_end_date_check_for_current_concepts
description: Active/valid concepts have valid_end_date = '2099-12-31' (the far-future sentinel). Expired concepts have a valid_end_date in the past. Queries building concept sets for current analysis should verify valid_end_date >= CURRENT_DATE or invalid_reason IS NULL.
logic: If concept is used to build a concept set (e.g., via WHERE concept_name LIKE or WHERE concept_code =) without checking invalid_reason IS NULL or valid_end_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT concept_id FROM concept WHERE concept_code = '250.00' AND vocabulary_id = 'ICD9CM' AND standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_relationship_replaces_for_deprecated_concepts
description: When a concept is deprecated (invalid_reason = 'U' for updated or 'D' for deleted), concept_relationship with relationship_id = 'Concept replaced by' points to the replacement concept. Queries should follow this chain for deprecated concept_ids.
logic: If a query uses a hardcoded concept_id that is known to be invalid (verifiable via concept.invalid_reason IS NOT NULL) without checking concept_relationship for 'Concept replaced by', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 4008576 -- Deprecated concept, no replacement lookup

name: concept_id_not_found_in_concept_table
description: All hardcoded concept_id values in a query should exist in the concept table. Using fabricated or misremembered concept_ids produces empty results without any SQL error.
logic: For each hardcoded integer used as a *_concept_id in a WHERE clause, verify it exists in concept.concept_id. If not found, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 999999999

name: concept_relationship_transitive_misuse
description: concept_relationship stores only direct (one-hop) relationships. To find all descendants across multiple levels, use concept_ancestor (which is pre-computed for all transitive hierarchical paths). Chaining multiple concept_relationship self-joins to simulate transitivity is fragile and incomplete.
logic: If a query chains 3+ self-joins on concept_relationship (cr1 -> cr2 -> cr3) to traverse a hierarchy, flag as warning and recommend concept_ancestor.
example_violation: SELECT cr3.concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship cr1 JOIN concept_relationship cr2 ON cr1.concept_id_2 = cr2.concept_id_1 JOIN concept_relationship cr3 ON cr2.concept_id_2 = cr3.concept_id_1 WHERE cr1.concept_id_1 = 201820 AND cr1.relationship_id = 'Subsumes' AND cr2.relationship_id = 'Subsumes' AND cr3.relationship_id = 'Subsumes'

name: standard_concept_filter_on_type_concept_id_lookup
description: When joining a *_type_concept_id column to concept to resolve its name, do NOT require standard_concept = 'S'. Type Concepts have their own domain ('Type Concept') and are always standard within that domain, but requiring standard_concept = 'S' is already correct. However, filtering by clinical domains (domain_id = 'Condition') on a type concept is wrong.
logic: If a *_type_concept_id column is joined to concept and the WHERE clause filters domain_id to a clinical domain (e.g., 'Condition', 'Drug') instead of 'Type Concept', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_type_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.domain_id = 'Condition'

name: concept_ancestor_includes_only_defines_ancestry_relationships
description: concept_ancestor is built only from relationships where relationship.defines_ancestry = 1. Not all hierarchical relationships produce ancestor paths. Queries should not assume that all 'Is a' chains appear in concept_ancestor if the vocabulary's relationships do not define ancestry.
logic: If a query expects concept_ancestor to contain hierarchical paths for a vocabulary whose relationships have defines_ancestry = 0, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 21600001 -- ATC class; ATC hierarchy uses concept_ancestor differently

name: concept_name_trailing_whitespace
description: Some concept_name values may have trailing whitespace in the vocabulary. Queries using exact equality on concept_name should use TRIM() or RTRIM() to avoid silent mismatches.
logic: If a query filters concept.concept_name with exact equality (=) rather than LIKE, and does not wrap concept_name in TRIM/RTRIM, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT concept_id FROM concept WHERE concept_name = 'Type 2 diabetes mellitus' AND standard_concept = 'S'

name: concept_ancestor_mixed_with_concept_relationship_redundantly
description: concept_ancestor already includes all transitive hierarchical paths. Joining concept_ancestor AND concept_relationship with 'Is a' in the same query for the same hierarchy traversal is redundant and may produce duplicated or incorrect row counts.
logic: If a query joins both concept_ancestor and concept_relationship (with 'Is a' or 'Subsumes') on the same concept chain for hierarchy traversal, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT DISTINCT ca.descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor ca JOIN concept_relationship cr ON ca.descendant_concept_id = cr.concept_id_1 WHERE ca.ancestor_concept_id = 201820 AND cr.relationship_id = 'Is a'

name: concept_class_id_ingredient_for_drug_grouping
description: When grouping drug data by active ingredient, filter concept_class_id = 'Ingredient' on the concept table. Using 'Clinical Drug', 'Branded Drug', or 'Clinical Drug Form' groups at the product/formulation level, not the ingredient level.
logic: If a query groups drug_exposure by drug_concept_id joined to concept and intends ingredient-level analysis (suggested by column aliases like 'ingredient', 'active_substance') but filters concept_class_id != 'Ingredient', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name AS ingredient, COUNT(*) FROM drug_exposure de JOIN concept c ON de.drug_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.concept_class_id = 'Clinical Drug' GROUP BY c.concept_name

name: concept_relationship_valid_date_range_check
description: concept_relationship has valid_start_date and valid_end_date. For current mappings, the row should have valid_end_date >= CURRENT_DATE (or the far-future sentinel '2099-12-31'). Relying solely on invalid_reason IS NULL without checking dates may include relationships that are technically scheduled to expire.
logic: If concept_relationship is queried with invalid_reason IS NULL but without checking valid_end_date >= CURRENT_DATE, flag as informational warning for temporal accuracy.
example_violation: SELECT concept_id_2 FROM concept_relationship WHERE concept_id_1 = 44836914 AND relationship_id = 'Maps to' AND invalid_reason IS NULL

name: person_no_clinical_event_dates
description: The person table has no clinical event date columns (no condition_start_date, drug_exposure_start_date, etc.). It only has birth-related temporal fields (year_of_birth, month_of_birth, day_of_birth, birth_datetime). Referencing event dates on person is a table confusion error.
logic: If a query references person.condition_start_date, person.drug_exposure_start_date, person.measurement_date, person.observation_date, or any clinical event date column on the person table, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT person_id, condition_start_date FROM person WHERE condition_start_date > '2020-01-01'

name: person_no_clinical_concept_id
description: The person table has only demographic concept_id columns (gender_concept_id, race_concept_id, ethnicity_concept_id). It does not have condition_concept_id, drug_concept_id, or any clinical event concept columns.
logic: If a query references person.condition_concept_id, person.drug_concept_id, person.procedure_concept_id, or person.measurement_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT person_id FROM person WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826

name: person_gender_domain_constraint
description: person.gender_concept_id must reference concepts from the Gender domain (domain_id = 'Gender'). The standard values are 8507 (Male), 8532 (Female), 8551 (Unknown), 8521 (Other). Using Condition, Drug, or other domain concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in person.gender_concept_id resolves to a concept with domain_id != 'Gender', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE gender_concept_id = 201826

name: person_race_domain_constraint
description: person.race_concept_id must reference concepts from the Race domain (domain_id = 'Race'). Using Gender or Ethnicity concept_ids in this column is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in person.race_concept_id resolves to a concept with domain_id != 'Race', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE race_concept_id = 38003563 -- This is an Ethnicity concept, not Race

name: person_ethnicity_domain_constraint
description: person.ethnicity_concept_id must reference concepts from the Ethnicity domain (domain_id = 'Ethnicity'). Standard values are 38003563 (Hispanic) and 38003564 (Not Hispanic). Using Race concept_ids is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in person.ethnicity_concept_id resolves to a concept with domain_id != 'Ethnicity', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE ethnicity_concept_id = 8527 -- This is a Race concept (White), not Ethnicity

name: person_year_of_birth_plausible_range
description: person.year_of_birth should be within a plausible range. Values before 1900 or after the current year indicate data quality issues or query logic errors.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters person.year_of_birth with a value < 1900 or > YEAR(CURRENT_DATE), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE year_of_birth = 1850

name: person_month_of_birth_valid_range
description: person.month_of_birth must be between 1 and 12. Values outside this range indicate a data quality issue or query logic error.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters person.month_of_birth with a value < 1 or > 12, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE month_of_birth = 13

name: person_day_of_birth_valid_range
description: person.day_of_birth must be between 1 and 31. Values outside this range are always invalid.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters person.day_of_birth with a value < 1 or > 31, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE day_of_birth = 32

name: condition_occurrence_domain_constraint
description: condition_occurrence.condition_concept_id must reference concepts from the Condition domain (domain_id = 'Condition'). This is enforced during ETL but should also be validated in analytical queries using hardcoded concept_ids.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in condition_occurrence.condition_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Condition', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 4215685 -- This is a Procedure concept

name: condition_occurrence_start_date_required_in_temporal_query
description: condition_start_date is a required NOT NULL column. Temporal queries on condition_occurrence should use condition_start_date, not condition_start_datetime (which may be NULL) or condition_end_date (which is nullable).
logic: If a query's primary temporal filter on condition_occurrence uses only condition_start_datetime or condition_end_date without fallback to condition_start_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_start_datetime BETWEEN '2023-01-01 00:00:00' AND '2023-12-31 23:59:59'

name: condition_occurrence_end_before_start
description: Queries should not construct filters where condition_end_date < condition_start_date for the same record. This is a logical impossibility and indicates a WHERE clause error.
logic: If a WHERE clause contains static conditions that force condition_end_date < condition_start_date for the same row, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_start_date > '2023-06-01' AND condition_end_date < '2023-01-01'

name: condition_occurrence_status_domain_constraint
description: condition_occurrence.condition_status_concept_id should use concepts representing diagnostic certainty (e.g., preliminary, final, admitting). These are typically in the Condition Status domain. Using clinical condition concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in condition_status_concept_id resolves to domain_id = 'Condition' or 'Drug' instead of a status/qualifier concept, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_status_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept, not a status

name: condition_occurrence_visit_detail_requires_visit_occurrence
description: When condition_occurrence.visit_detail_id is used to link to visit_detail, the query should also be aware that visit_detail nests inside visit_occurrence. If the query needs visit-level attributes, join through visit_occurrence_id, not only visit_detail_id.
logic: If a query joins condition_occurrence to visit_detail on visit_detail_id but also references visit_occurrence columns without joining through visit_occurrence, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT co.*, vo.visit_start_date FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN visit_detail vd ON co.visit_detail_id = vd.visit_detail_id -- vo referenced but not joined

name: drug_exposure_domain_constraint
description: drug_exposure.drug_concept_id must reference concepts from the Drug domain (domain_id = 'Drug'). Using Condition, Procedure, or Device concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in drug_exposure.drug_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Drug', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE drug_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept

name: drug_exposure_start_date_required_in_temporal_query
description: drug_exposure_start_date is a required NOT NULL column and the most reliable temporal anchor. drug_exposure_end_date and drug_exposure_start_datetime may be NULL. Always use start_date as the primary temporal filter.
logic: If a temporal query on drug_exposure uses only drug_exposure_end_date or drug_exposure_start_datetime as the sole temporal filter without drug_exposure_start_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE drug_exposure_end_date > '2023-01-01'

name: drug_exposure_days_supply_plausible_range
description: drug_exposure.days_supply should be a positive integer in a plausible range (typically 1–365). Values <= 0 or > 365 may indicate data quality issues or query construction errors.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters days_supply with a value <= 0 or > 365, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE days_supply = -30

name: drug_exposure_route_concept_domain_constraint
description: drug_exposure.route_concept_id must reference concepts from the Route domain (e.g., 4132161=Oral, 4171047=Intravenous, 4262099=Topical). Using Drug or Condition domain concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in drug_exposure.route_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Route', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE route_concept_id = 1125315 -- Drug concept used as route

name: drug_exposure_days_supply_inconsistent_with_dates
description: If a query simultaneously filters on days_supply AND computes duration from drug_exposure_start_date to drug_exposure_end_date, the two values should be consistent. A query that filters days_supply = 30 but DATEDIFF returns 90 indicates a logic or data quality issue.
logic: If a query filters on both days_supply and a DATEDIFF(drug_exposure_start_date, drug_exposure_end_date) in conflicting ranges, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE days_supply = 30 AND DATEDIFF(day, drug_exposure_start_date, drug_exposure_end_date) > 90

name: drug_exposure_quantity_negative_value
description: drug_exposure.quantity should be a non-negative number representing the amount dispensed. Filtering with negative values is almost always incorrect.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters drug_exposure.quantity < 0, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE quantity = -10

name: procedure_occurrence_domain_constraint
description: procedure_occurrence.procedure_concept_id must reference concepts from the Procedure domain (domain_id = 'Procedure'). Using Condition or Drug domain concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in procedure_occurrence.procedure_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Procedure', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM procedure_occurrence WHERE procedure_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept

name: procedure_occurrence_modifier_domain_constraint
description: procedure_occurrence.modifier_concept_id should reference concepts from the Modifier domain (e.g., bilateral, left, right, professional component). Using Procedure or Condition domain concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in modifier_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Modifier' (or 'Observation' for some laterality qualifiers), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM procedure_occurrence WHERE modifier_concept_id = 2213473 -- This is a Procedure concept, not a Modifier

name: procedure_occurrence_end_date_nullable
description: procedure_occurrence.procedure_end_date is nullable — many procedures are single-point-in-time events. Duration calculations using DATEDIFF(procedure_date, procedure_end_date) must handle NULLs.
logic: If a query computes DATEDIFF or arithmetic on procedure_end_date without COALESCE, ISNULL, or IS NOT NULL filter, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT DATEDIFF(day, procedure_date, procedure_end_date) AS duration FROM procedure_occurrence

name: procedure_occurrence_quantity_semantics
description: procedure_occurrence.quantity indicates the number of times a procedure was performed in a single record (e.g., 2 units of physical therapy). It is not the number of procedure records for a patient. Do not confuse it with COUNT(*).
logic: If a query uses SUM(quantity) aliased as 'procedure_count' or 'number_of_procedures' when the intent appears to be counting records (not summing units), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT person_id, SUM(quantity) AS procedure_count FROM procedure_occurrence GROUP BY person_id

name: measurement_domain_constraint
description: measurement.measurement_concept_id must reference concepts from the Measurement domain (domain_id = 'Measurement'). Using Condition or Observation domain concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in measurement.measurement_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Measurement', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept

name: measurement_unit_domain_constraint
description: measurement.unit_concept_id must reference concepts from the Unit domain (domain_id = 'Unit'). These are typically UCUM units. Using Measurement or Drug concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in measurement.unit_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Unit', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE unit_concept_id = 3004249 -- Measurement concept used as unit

name: measurement_operator_domain_constraint
description: measurement.operator_concept_id must reference operator concepts: 4171756 (<), 4172704 (>), 4171755 (=), 4171754 (<=), 4172703 (>=). Using any other concept_id is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in operator_concept_id is not one of {4171756, 4172704, 4171755, 4171754, 4172703}, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE operator_concept_id = 201826

name: measurement_range_low_greater_than_range_high
description: If a query filters measurement records where range_low > range_high, this is logically impossible for valid reference ranges and indicates a data quality issue or a WHERE clause error.
logic: If a WHERE clause contains range_low > range_high as a positive filter (not a DQ check), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE range_low > range_high AND value_as_number > range_high

name: measurement_value_as_number_and_concept_exclusive
description: In measurement, value_as_number (quantitative) and value_as_concept_id (qualitative) typically represent the same result in different forms. Filtering both with AND rarely matches any records. Use OR or check the appropriate one for the measurement type.
logic: If a WHERE clause applies AND conditions on both measurement.value_as_number and measurement.value_as_concept_id (non-NULL checks excluded), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE value_as_number > 6.5 AND value_as_concept_id = 45884084

name: measurement_no_value_as_string
description: Unlike the observation table, measurement does NOT have a value_as_string column. Referencing measurement.value_as_string is a column error. Measurement results are stored in value_as_number or value_as_concept_id.
logic: If a query references measurement.value_as_string, flag as error — this column exists only on observation.
example_violation: SELECT value_as_string FROM measurement WHERE measurement_concept_id = 3004249

name: measurement_date_required_in_temporal_query
description: measurement_date is a required NOT NULL column. measurement_datetime and measurement_time are optional. Use measurement_date as the primary temporal filter for reliability.
logic: If a temporal query on measurement uses only measurement_datetime or measurement_time as the sole filter without measurement_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_datetime > '2023-01-01 08:00:00'

name: observation_domain_constraint
description: observation.observation_concept_id must reference concepts from the Observation domain (domain_id = 'Observation'). Using Measurement, Condition, or Drug domain concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in observation.observation_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Observation', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE observation_concept_id = 3004249 -- Measurement concept in observation

name: observation_unit_domain_constraint
description: observation.unit_concept_id, when populated, must reference concepts from the Unit domain. Using observation or measurement concepts as units is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in observation.unit_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Unit', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE unit_concept_id = 4058286 -- Observation concept used as unit

name: observation_qualifier_domain_constraint
description: observation.qualifier_concept_id provides context (severity, laterality, etc.) and should reference qualifier-type concepts. Using primary clinical concepts (Condition, Drug) is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in qualifier_concept_id resolves to domain_id IN ('Condition', 'Drug', 'Procedure'), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE qualifier_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept used as qualifier

name: observation_value_as_concept_domain_constraint
description: observation.value_as_concept_id stores the result of the observation as a concept. It should reference concepts from the 'Meas Value' domain or other answer-set domains, not clinical event concepts used for observation_concept_id.
logic: If value_as_concept_id is filtered with the same concept_id used in observation_concept_id (confusing the question with the answer), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE observation_concept_id = 4058286 AND value_as_concept_id = 4058286 -- Same concept as both question and answer

name: observation_date_required_in_temporal_query
description: observation_date is a required NOT NULL column. observation_datetime is optional. Use observation_date for primary temporal filtering.
logic: If a temporal query on observation uses only observation_datetime without observation_date as a filter, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE observation_datetime > '2023-01-01 00:00:00'

name: observation_no_range_low_range_high
description: Unlike measurement, the observation table does not have range_low or range_high columns. Referencing these on observation is a column error.
logic: If a query references observation.range_low or observation.range_high, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE value_as_number > range_high

name: observation_no_operator_concept_id
description: Unlike measurement, the observation table does not have an operator_concept_id column. Referencing it on observation is a column error.
logic: If a query references observation.operator_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE operator_concept_id = 4171756

name: visit_occurrence_domain_constraint
description: visit_occurrence.visit_concept_id must reference concepts from the Visit domain (domain_id = 'Visit'). Standard visit concepts include: 9201 (Inpatient), 9202 (Outpatient), 9203 (Emergency Room), 262 (ER+Inpatient), 9201 (Inpatient). Using Condition or Procedure concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in visit_occurrence.visit_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Visit', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept used as visit type

name: visit_occurrence_inpatient_end_date_required
description: For inpatient visits (visit_concept_id = 9201), visit_end_date represents the discharge date and is critical for length-of-stay calculations. Queries computing inpatient LOS should verify visit_end_date IS NOT NULL.
logic: If a query computes DATEDIFF(visit_start_date, visit_end_date) for inpatient visits without handling NULL visit_end_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT DATEDIFF(day, visit_start_date, visit_end_date) AS los FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_concept_id = 9201

name: visit_occurrence_outpatient_same_day
description: Outpatient visits (visit_concept_id = 9202) typically have visit_start_date = visit_end_date (same-day). Queries filtering outpatient visits with multi-day date ranges may indicate confusion with inpatient logic.
logic: If a query filters outpatient visits (visit_concept_id = 9202) with DATEDIFF(visit_start_date, visit_end_date) > 1, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_concept_id = 9202 AND DATEDIFF(day, visit_start_date, visit_end_date) > 30

name: visit_occurrence_event_before_visit_start
description: When linking clinical events to visits, the event date should fall within the visit date range. An event date before visit_start_date indicates a join or data quality error.
logic: If a query joins a clinical event to visit_occurrence and includes a WHERE clause where the event date < visit_start_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON co.visit_occurrence_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id WHERE co.condition_start_date < vo.visit_start_date

name: visit_occurrence_no_clinical_values
description: visit_occurrence stores visit metadata only. It has no value columns (no value_as_number, value_as_concept_id, value_as_string, unit_concept_id, quantity). These belong to measurement, observation, or clinical event tables.
logic: If a query references visit_occurrence.value_as_number, visit_occurrence.value_as_string, visit_occurrence.unit_concept_id, or visit_occurrence.quantity, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT value_as_number FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_concept_id = 9201

name: visit_detail_domain_constraint
description: visit_detail.visit_detail_concept_id must reference concepts from the Visit domain (domain_id = 'Visit'), similar to visit_occurrence.visit_concept_id. These represent sub-visit types (ICU stay, ward transfer, etc.).
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in visit_detail.visit_detail_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Visit', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept

name: visit_detail_must_reference_visit_occurrence
description: Every visit_detail record must have a visit_occurrence_id linking it to its parent visit. Queries analyzing visit_detail should join to visit_occurrence to access visit-level context (visit type, dates). A visit_detail without this join is missing critical context.
logic: If a query selects from visit_detail but never references visit_occurrence (no join, no subquery), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT person_id, visit_detail_start_date FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_concept_id = 32037

name: visit_detail_end_before_start
description: visit_detail_end_date should never be before visit_detail_start_date. A query constructing WHERE conditions that force this is logically impossible.
logic: If a WHERE clause contains static conditions forcing visit_detail_end_date < visit_detail_start_date for the same row, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_start_date > '2023-06-01' AND visit_detail_end_date < '2023-01-01'

name: visit_detail_no_preceding_visit_occurrence_id
description: visit_detail has preceding_visit_detail_id for temporal chaining within visit_detail, and visit_occurrence_id for linking to the parent visit. It does NOT have preceding_visit_occurrence_id — that column exists only on visit_occurrence.
logic: If a query references visit_detail.preceding_visit_occurrence_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT preceding_visit_occurrence_id FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_id = 100

name: visit_detail_dates_within_parent_visit
description: visit_detail dates should fall within the parent visit_occurrence date range. A query that joins visit_detail to visit_occurrence and filters visit_detail dates outside the visit range indicates a logic error.
logic: If a query joins visit_detail to visit_occurrence and includes a WHERE clause where visit_detail_start_date < visit_start_date or visit_detail_end_date > visit_end_date, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail vd JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON vd.visit_occurrence_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id WHERE vd.visit_detail_start_date < vo.visit_start_date

name: death_no_visit_occurrence_id
description: The death table does not have visit_occurrence_id, visit_detail_id, provider_id, care_site_id, or any clinical event FK other than person_id. To link death to a visit, join both tables to person independently and use temporal logic.
logic: If a query references death.visit_occurrence_id, death.provider_id, death.care_site_id, or death.visit_detail_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT visit_occurrence_id FROM death WHERE person_id = 12345

name: death_cause_concept_condition_domain
description: death.cause_concept_id should reference standard concepts from the Condition domain, since causes of death are conditions. Using Drug, Procedure, or Measurement concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in death.cause_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Condition', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death WHERE cause_concept_id = 1125315 -- Drug concept as cause of death

name: death_date_not_before_birth
description: death.death_date must logically be on or after the person's birth date. A query that could produce results where death_date < birth_datetime (or death year < year_of_birth) indicates a data quality issue or join error.
logic: If a query joins death to person and includes a WHERE clause where death_date < person's birth (YEAR(death_date) < year_of_birth), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT d.* FROM death d JOIN person p ON d.person_id = p.person_id WHERE YEAR(d.death_date) < p.year_of_birth

name: death_date_in_future
description: death.death_date should not be in the future relative to the data extraction date. Filtering for death_date > CURRENT_DATE indicates a query logic error or data corruption.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters death.death_date > CURRENT_DATE (or a future date literal), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death WHERE death_date > '2030-01-01'

name: death_cause_source_concept_not_standard
description: death.cause_source_concept_id stores the source (non-standard) concept for the cause of death. It should not be filtered with standard_concept = 'S'. Use cause_concept_id for standard concept filtering.
logic: If a query filters death.cause_source_concept_id and then joins to concept with standard_concept = 'S', flag as error — source concepts are non-standard by definition.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death d JOIN concept c ON d.cause_source_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.standard_concept = 'S'

name: clinical_event_date_in_future
description: Clinical event dates (condition_start_date, drug_exposure_start_date, procedure_date, measurement_date, observation_date) should not be in the future. Filtering with future dates may indicate a query construction error.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters a clinical event start date > CURRENT_DATE or a far-future literal (e.g., > '2030-01-01'), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_start_date > '2030-01-01'

name: clinical_event_date_before_1900
description: Clinical event dates before 1900 are almost always data quality issues. Querying with such dates may indicate an error in date literal formatting or a test-data artifact.
logic: If a WHERE clause filters a clinical event date < '1900-01-01', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE drug_exposure_start_date < '1800-01-01'

name: all_clinical_tables_require_person_id_for_patient_query
description: When combining data across multiple clinical tables for a patient-level analysis, all tables must be linked through person_id (directly or transitively). Missing person_id linkage produces cross-patient contamination in results.
logic: If a query joins two or more clinical tables without any person_id linkage (direct or transitive through intermediate tables), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT co.condition_concept_id, de.drug_concept_id FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN drug_exposure de ON co.condition_start_date = de.drug_exposure_start_date

name: clinical_to_provider_join_key
description: Clinical event tables (condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, procedure_occurrence, measurement, observation) join to the provider table via provider_id on both sides. Joining on person_id, care_site_id, or any other column is incorrect.
logic: If a clinical event table is joined to provider on any column other than provider_id = provider_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN provider p ON co.person_id = p.provider_id

name: clinical_to_care_site_join_key
