description: Clinical event tables and visit_occurrence join to care_site via care_site_id on both sides. Joining on location_id, provider_id, or care_site_name is incorrect.
logic: If any table is joined to care_site on a column other than care_site_id = care_site_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence vo JOIN care_site cs ON vo.care_site_id = cs.location_id

name: care_site_to_location_join_key
description: care_site joins to location via care_site.location_id = location.location_id. Joining on care_site_id, care_site_name, or care_site_source_value is incorrect.
logic: If care_site is joined to location on any column other than location_id = location_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM care_site cs JOIN location l ON cs.care_site_id = l.location_id

name: person_to_location_join_key
description: person joins to location via person.location_id = location.location_id to get the patient's address. Joining on person_id, person_source_value, or care_site_id is incorrect.
logic: If person is joined to location on any column other than location_id = location_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person p JOIN location l ON p.person_id = l.location_id

name: provider_to_care_site_join_key
description: provider joins to care_site via provider.care_site_id = care_site.care_site_id to get the provider's practice location. Joining on provider_id or specialty_concept_id is incorrect.
logic: If provider is joined to care_site on any column other than care_site_id = care_site_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM provider p JOIN care_site cs ON p.provider_id = cs.care_site_id

name: visit_detail_to_visit_occurrence_join_key
description: visit_detail joins to visit_occurrence via visit_detail.visit_occurrence_id = visit_occurrence.visit_occurrence_id. This is the FK that nests visit_detail within its parent visit. Joining only on person_id produces a Cartesian product across all visits.
logic: If visit_detail is joined to visit_occurrence and the ON clause does not include visit_occurrence_id = visit_occurrence_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail vd JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON vd.person_id = vo.person_id

name: clinical_to_visit_detail_join_key
description: Clinical event tables join to visit_detail via visit_detail_id on both sides. Joining on visit_occurrence_id to visit_detail.visit_detail_id is a type mismatch that silently produces wrong results.
logic: If a clinical event table's visit_occurrence_id is joined to visit_detail.visit_detail_id (or vice versa), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement m JOIN visit_detail vd ON m.visit_occurrence_id = vd.visit_detail_id

name: concept_primary_concept_id_join_column
description: All joins TO the concept table must target concept.concept_id (the primary key). Joining to concept.concept_name, concept.concept_code, concept.vocabulary_id, or concept.domain_id as the join predicate is incorrect.
logic: If any table joins to concept on a column other than concept.concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure de JOIN concept c ON de.drug_source_value = c.concept_name

name: source_concept_id_to_concept_join_separate_alias
description: When a clinical table joins to concept for both the primary *_concept_id AND the *_source_concept_id, each join must use a separate alias for the concept table. Reusing the same alias corrupts the ON clause for one of the joins.

required_keys.note: Each concept join must have a unique alias

forbidden_keys: Same alias for both standard and source concept joins
logic: If a query joins the same table to concept twice (once for *_concept_id, once for *_source_concept_id) and both JOIN clauses use the same alias, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_concept_id = c.concept_id JOIN concept c ON co.condition_source_concept_id = c.concept_id

name: type_concept_id_join_requires_own_alias
description: When joining *_type_concept_id to concept alongside the primary *_concept_id, the type concept join must use its own distinct alias. The type concept resolves to provenance information ('EHR', 'Claim'), not clinical meaning.

required_keys.note: Separate alias for type concept join (e.g., c_type)

forbidden_keys: Same alias as the primary concept join
logic: If *_type_concept_id is joined to concept using the same alias already used for the primary *_concept_id join, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name AS condition_name, c.concept_name AS type_name FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept c ON co.condition_concept_id = c.concept_id JOIN concept c ON co.condition_type_concept_id = c.concept_id

name: concept_to_vocabulary_join_key
description: concept joins to vocabulary via concept.vocabulary_id = vocabulary.vocabulary_id (VARCHAR to VARCHAR). Joining on concept_id = vocabulary_concept_id or vocabulary_id = vocabulary_name is incorrect.
logic: If concept is joined to vocabulary on any column pair other than vocabulary_id = vocabulary_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN vocabulary v ON c.concept_id = v.vocabulary_concept_id

name: concept_to_domain_join_key
description: concept joins to domain via concept.domain_id = domain.domain_id (VARCHAR to VARCHAR). Joining on concept_id = domain_concept_id is incorrect.
logic: If concept is joined to domain on any column pair other than domain_id = domain_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN domain d ON c.concept_id = d.domain_concept_id

name: concept_to_concept_class_join_key
description: concept joins to concept_class via concept.concept_class_id = concept_class.concept_class_id (VARCHAR to VARCHAR). Joining on concept_id = concept_class_concept_id is incorrect.
logic: If concept is joined to concept_class on any column pair other than concept_class_id = concept_class_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept c JOIN concept_class cc ON c.concept_id = cc.concept_class_concept_id

name: concept_relationship_to_relationship_join_key
description: concept_relationship joins to the relationship table via concept_relationship.relationship_id = relationship.relationship_id (VARCHAR to VARCHAR). Joining on concept_id columns is incorrect.
logic: If concept_relationship is joined to relationship on any column other than relationship_id = relationship_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept_relationship cr JOIN relationship r ON cr.concept_id_1 = r.relationship_concept_id

name: concept_ancestor_to_concept_descendant_side
description: When using concept_ancestor to expand a concept set for clinical table filtering, the clinical table's *_concept_id must join to concept_ancestor.descendant_concept_id (not ancestor_concept_id). The anchor concept goes into ancestor_concept_id via WHERE.
logic: If a clinical table's *_concept_id is joined to concept_ancestor.ancestor_concept_id (and the WHERE filters descendant_concept_id), flag as error — the join direction is inverted.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN concept_ancestor ca ON co.condition_concept_id = ca.ancestor_concept_id WHERE ca.descendant_concept_id = 201820

name: concept_ancestor_to_concept_for_name_resolution
description: When resolving descendant concept names from concept_ancestor, join concept_ancestor.descendant_concept_id to concept.concept_id. Joining ancestor_concept_id to concept resolves the parent's name, not the descendant's.

required_keys.note: Match the correct side: descendant_concept_id -> concept.concept_id for descendant names; ancestor_concept_id -> concept.concept_id for ancestor names

forbidden_keys: Joining the wrong side for the intended resolution
logic: If a query joins concept_ancestor to concept and SELECTs concept.concept_name, verify the join uses the correct concept_ancestor column for the intended resolution. If aliased as 'descendant_name' but joined on ancestor_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c.concept_name AS descendant_name FROM concept_ancestor ca JOIN concept c ON ca.ancestor_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE ca.descendant_concept_id = 201826

name: concept_relationship_concept_id_1_to_concept
description: concept_relationship.concept_id_1 joins to concept.concept_id for the source concept. concept_relationship.concept_id_2 joins to a second concept alias for the target concept. Swapping which side joins to which alias corrupts the mapping direction.

required_keys.note: concept_id_1 -> source concept alias; concept_id_2 -> target concept alias

logic: If concept_relationship is joined to two concept aliases and the aliases are swapped (concept_id_1 goes to the alias used for target attributes, concept_id_2 to the source alias), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT c_source.concept_name AS source_name, c_target.concept_name AS target_name FROM concept_relationship cr JOIN concept c_source ON cr.concept_id_2 = c_source.concept_id JOIN concept c_target ON cr.concept_id_1 = c_target.concept_id WHERE cr.relationship_id = 'Maps to'

name: drug_exposure_to_drug_strength_join_key
description: drug_exposure joins to drug_strength via drug_exposure.drug_concept_id = drug_strength.drug_concept_id. Joining on drug_exposure_id, drug_source_value, or person_id is incorrect — drug_strength has no such columns.
logic: If drug_exposure is joined to drug_strength on any column other than drug_concept_id = drug_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure de JOIN drug_strength ds ON de.drug_exposure_id = ds.drug_concept_id

name: note_nlp_to_note_join_key
description: note_nlp joins to note via note_nlp.note_id = note.note_id. note_nlp has no person_id, visit_occurrence_id, or provider_id columns. Patient-level data must be accessed through the note table.
logic: If note_nlp is joined to note on any column other than note_id = note_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note_nlp nn JOIN note n ON nn.note_nlp_id = n.note_id

name: clinical_tables_forbidden_direct_join_to_location
description: Clinical event tables (condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, procedure_occurrence, measurement, observation) do not have a location_id column. To get location data for a clinical event, join through care_site (event.care_site_id -> care_site.care_site_id -> location) or through person (event.person_id -> person.location_id -> location).

required_keys.note: Must go through care_site or person as intermediate table

forbidden_keys: Any direct join from clinical event table to location
logic: If a clinical event table is joined directly to location on any column, flag as error — these tables have no location_id FK.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN location l ON co.visit_occurrence_id = l.location_id

name: death_forbidden_join_to_visit_on_non_person_id
description: The death table's only FK to other tables is person_id. Joining death to visit_occurrence on death_date = visit_start_date or any column other than person_id is incorrect structurally, even if temporally meaningful.
logic: If death is joined to visit_occurrence on any column other than person_id = person_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death d JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON d.death_date = vo.visit_end_date

name: cohort_to_clinical_table_via_subject_id_to_person_id
description: The cohort table uses subject_id (not person_id) to identify patients. When joining cohort to any clinical table, use cohort.subject_id = clinical_table.person_id. Joining subject_id to visit_occurrence_id, condition_occurrence_id, or other PKs is incorrect.
join_to: condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, procedure_occurrence, measurement, observation, visit_occurrence
logic: If cohort is joined to a clinical table and subject_id is matched to any column other than person_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM cohort c JOIN condition_occurrence co ON c.subject_id = co.condition_occurrence_id

name: observation_period_to_clinical_requires_person_id_and_dates
description: observation_period joins to clinical tables via person_id AND a date overlap constraint. A join on person_id alone produces multiple observation_period rows per event (since patients can have multiple observation periods), inflating results.
forbidden_keys: person_id as sole join key without date constraint
logic: If a clinical event table is joined to observation_period on person_id alone without a date overlap condition in ON or WHERE, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT co.* FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN observation_period op ON co.person_id = op.person_id

name: era_table_forbidden_join_to_visit_occurrence
description: Era tables (condition_era, drug_era, dose_era) have no visit_occurrence_id, visit_detail_id, or provider_id columns. They can only be joined to person (on person_id) and concept (on *_concept_id). Joining to visit_occurrence is impossible.
forbidden_keys: Any column — no FK relationship exists between era tables and visit_occurrence
logic: If any era table is joined to visit_occurrence, visit_detail, provider, or care_site on any column, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_era de JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON de.person_id = vo.person_id AND de.drug_era_start_date = vo.visit_start_date

name: cost_to_clinical_table_requires_event_id_and_domain
description: cost links to clinical tables via cost.cost_event_id = clinical_table.primary_key AND cost.cost_domain_id = the domain string. Joining only on cost_event_id without filtering cost_domain_id can match IDs across tables (a drug_exposure_id could equal a condition_occurrence_id).
join_to: condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, procedure_occurrence, measurement, observation, visit_occurrence
logic: If cost is joined to a clinical table on cost_event_id = primary_key but cost_domain_id is not filtered to the appropriate domain, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM cost c JOIN procedure_occurrence po ON c.cost_event_id = po.procedure_occurrence_id -- Missing: WHERE c.cost_domain_id = 'Procedure'

name: person_id_cross_matched_to_non_person_id_pk
description: person_id columns across all tables reference person.person_id. Joining a person_id column to another table's primary key (e.g., visit_occurrence_id, condition_occurrence_id, measurement_id) is always wrong — these are different ID spaces.
logic: If person_id from any table is joined to a column that is not person_id on the target table, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON co.person_id = vo.visit_occurrence_id

name: visit_occurrence_id_cross_matched_to_non_visit_id
description: visit_occurrence_id columns reference visit_occurrence.visit_occurrence_id. Joining visit_occurrence_id to person_id, condition_occurrence_id, or visit_detail_id is always wrong.
logic: If a clinical table's visit_occurrence_id is joined to a column that is not visit_occurrence.visit_occurrence_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure de JOIN visit_occurrence vo ON de.visit_occurrence_id = vo.person_id

name: forbidden_clinical_to_clinical_pk_cross_join
description: Clinical event table primary keys (condition_occurrence_id, drug_exposure_id, procedure_occurrence_id, measurement_id, observation_id) are table-specific and must never be joined to each other. They are independent ID sequences.

required_keys.note: Clinical tables link via person_id and/or visit_occurrence_id, never via cross-table PKs

logic: If one clinical event table's primary key is joined to another clinical event table's primary key, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN drug_exposure de ON co.condition_occurrence_id = de.drug_exposure_id

name: concept_synonym_to_concept_join_key
description: concept_synonym joins to concept via concept_synonym.concept_id = concept.concept_id. Joining on concept_synonym_name to concept.concept_name is incorrect and produces unreliable results because synonyms are alternative names.
logic: If concept_synonym is joined to concept on concept_synonym_name = concept_name or any column other than concept_id = concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM concept_synonym cs JOIN concept c ON cs.concept_synonym_name = c.concept_name

name: payer_plan_period_to_clinical_requires_person_id_and_dates
description: payer_plan_period links to clinical events via person_id AND a date overlap. Joining only on person_id without date constraints pairs events with incorrect payer periods (a patient may have multiple insurance periods).
logic: If a clinical event table is joined to payer_plan_period on person_id alone without a date overlap condition, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure de JOIN payer_plan_period pp ON de.person_id = pp.person_id

name: fact_relationship_requires_domain_concept_ids
description: fact_relationship is a polymorphic table linking any two facts across the CDM. It has domain_concept_id_1 and domain_concept_id_2 to identify which tables fact_id_1 and fact_id_2 reference, plus relationship_concept_id for the relationship type. Queries must filter all three concept_id columns; without domain_concept_ids, fact_id values collide across tables.
logic: If fact_relationship is queried or joined without filtering domain_concept_id_1, domain_concept_id_2, and relationship_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM fact_relationship WHERE fact_id_1 = 12345
example_correct: SELECT * FROM fact_relationship WHERE domain_concept_id_1 = 21 AND domain_concept_id_2 = 27 AND relationship_concept_id = 44818757 AND fact_id_1 = 12345

name: fact_relationship_fact_id_polymorphic_join
description: fact_relationship.fact_id_1 and fact_id_2 are polymorphic FKs. fact_id_1 references the PK of the table identified by domain_concept_id_1, and fact_id_2 references the PK identified by domain_concept_id_2. Joining fact_id_1 to a clinical table without filtering domain_concept_id_1 may match wrong-table IDs.
logic: If fact_relationship.fact_id_1 or fact_id_2 is joined to a clinical table's PK without the corresponding domain_concept_id being filtered to match that table's domain, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM fact_relationship fr JOIN measurement m ON fr.fact_id_1 = m.measurement_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM fact_relationship fr JOIN measurement m ON fr.fact_id_1 = m.measurement_id WHERE fr.domain_concept_id_1 = 21 -- 21 = Measurement domain concept

name: fact_relationship_relationship_concept_id_required
description: fact_relationship.relationship_concept_id identifies the nature of the link between two facts. Without it, the query conflates all relationship types. This column must always be filtered or grouped.
logic: If fact_relationship is used without filtering or grouping by relationship_concept_id, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT fact_id_1, fact_id_2 FROM fact_relationship WHERE domain_concept_id_1 = 21 AND domain_concept_id_2 = 27
example_correct: SELECT fact_id_1, fact_id_2 FROM fact_relationship WHERE domain_concept_id_1 = 21 AND domain_concept_id_2 = 27 AND relationship_concept_id = 44818757

name: delete_truncate_on_clinical_tables
description: Clinical event tables (condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, procedure_occurrence, measurement, observation, visit_occurrence, visit_detail, death, person) contain patient-level data. Analytical queries should never issue DELETE, UPDATE, INSERT, TRUNCATE, or DROP against them.
logic: If the SQL statement contains DELETE, UPDATE, INSERT, TRUNCATE, or DROP targeting any clinical event table or the person table, flag as error.
example_violation: DELETE FROM measurement WHERE measurement_date < '2010-01-01'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_date < '2010-01-01'

name: between_inclusive_both_ends_with_datetime
description: SQL BETWEEN is inclusive on both ends. When used with date columns, 'BETWEEN 2023-01-01 AND 2023-01-31' includes records on both Jan 1 and Jan 31. But with datetime columns, 'BETWEEN 2023-01-01 AND 2023-01-31' excludes any records after midnight on Jan 31 (e.g., 2023-01-31 08:00:00). Use < '2023-02-01' for end-of-month datetime ranges.
logic: If a *_datetime column is filtered with BETWEEN using date literals (no time component), flag as warning — records with non-midnight times on the end date will be excluded.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_datetime BETWEEN '2023-01-01' AND '2023-01-31'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE measurement_datetime >= '2023-01-01' AND measurement_datetime < '2023-02-01'

name: not_in_subquery_with_nullable_column
description: NOT IN (subquery) returns no rows if the subquery returns any NULL values, because NULL comparisons are always UNKNOWN. In OMOP, nullable FK columns like visit_occurrence_id, provider_id, and visit_detail_id often contain NULLs. Use NOT EXISTS or add IS NOT NULL to the subquery instead.
logic: If a WHERE clause uses NOT IN with a subquery that selects a nullable column (visit_occurrence_id, provider_id, visit_detail_id, care_site_id), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE person_id NOT IN (SELECT visit_occurrence_id FROM condition_occurrence)
example_correct: SELECT * FROM person p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM condition_occurrence co WHERE co.person_id = p.person_id)

name: note_encoding_concept_id_domain
description: note.encoding_concept_id specifies the character encoding of note_text (e.g., UTF-8, ASCII). It must reference concepts representing encodings, not clinical concepts. Using Condition, Drug, or Measurement concepts is incorrect.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in note.encoding_concept_id resolves to a clinical domain (Condition, Drug, Procedure, Measurement), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note WHERE encoding_concept_id = 201826
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note WHERE encoding_concept_id = 32678 -- UTF-8 encoding concept

name: note_language_concept_id_domain
description: note.language_concept_id specifies the language of the note (e.g., English, Spanish). It must reference language concepts, not clinical concepts.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in note.language_concept_id resolves to a clinical domain rather than a language concept, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note WHERE language_concept_id = 1125315 -- Drug concept used as language
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note WHERE language_concept_id = 4180186 -- English

name: note_title_not_for_clinical_filtering
description: note.note_title is a free-text field for the document title (e.g., 'Discharge Summary', 'Progress Note'). Using it with LIKE for clinical concept discovery is unreliable. Use note_class_concept_id for note category and note_type_concept_id for provenance.
logic: If note.note_title is used with LIKE to identify clinical categories (e.g., '%discharge%', '%radiology%') instead of note_class_concept_id, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note WHERE note_title LIKE '%Discharge%'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note WHERE note_class_concept_id = 44814637 -- Discharge Summary concept

name: note_source_value_not_for_filtering
description: note.note_source_value stores the source-system identifier for the note. It is site-specific and non-standardized. Use note_class_concept_id or note_type_concept_id for analytical filtering.
logic: If note.note_source_value is used in WHERE for analytical filtering (not data quality), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note WHERE note_source_value = 'DIS_SUMM'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note WHERE note_class_concept_id = 44814637

name: note_nlp_section_concept_id_domain
description: note_nlp.section_concept_id identifies the section of the clinical note where the NLP extraction occurred (e.g., History of Present Illness, Assessment & Plan). It should reference section-type concepts, not clinical condition or drug concepts.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in note_nlp.section_concept_id resolves to domain_id IN ('Condition', 'Drug', 'Procedure', 'Measurement'), flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE section_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept used as section
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE section_concept_id = 35850000 -- History of Present Illness section

name: note_nlp_offset_is_character_position
description: note_nlp.offset is a VARCHAR field storing the character position of the NLP extraction within the note text. It is not an integer ID and should not be used in JOINs or numeric comparisons without explicit CAST.
logic: If note_nlp.offset is used in a JOIN, or in numeric comparison without CAST/CONVERT, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE offset > 100
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE CAST(offset AS INT) > 100

name: note_nlp_nlp_system_for_provenance
description: note_nlp.nlp_system identifies which NLP pipeline produced the extraction (e.g., 'cTAKES', 'MedSpacy'). When multiple NLP systems have processed the same notes, queries should filter nlp_system to avoid duplicate/conflicting extractions.
logic: If note_nlp is queried with a concept filter but without filtering or grouping by nlp_system, and the CDM is known to have multiple NLP pipelines, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT DISTINCT n.person_id FROM note_nlp nn JOIN note n ON nn.note_id = n.note_id WHERE nn.note_nlp_concept_id = 201826 AND nn.term_exists = 'Y'
example_correct: SELECT DISTINCT n.person_id FROM note_nlp nn JOIN note n ON nn.note_id = n.note_id WHERE nn.note_nlp_concept_id = 201826 AND nn.term_exists = 'Y' AND nn.nlp_system = 'cTAKES'

name: note_nlp_term_modifiers_is_free_text
description: note_nlp.term_modifiers is a free-text field storing additional NLP contextual attributes (e.g., 'subject=patient, certainty=positive'). It should not be used in JOINs to concept or treated as a concept_id.
logic: If note_nlp.term_modifiers is used in a JOIN to concept or cast to integer, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note_nlp nn JOIN concept c ON nn.term_modifiers = c.concept_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE term_modifiers LIKE '%certainty=positive%'

name: note_nlp_nlp_date_for_temporal_filtering
description: note_nlp.nlp_date is the date the NLP processing was performed, NOT the clinical date of the extracted concept. To get the clinical date, join to note.note_date via note_id. Filtering nlp_date as if it were the event date is incorrect.
logic: If note_nlp.nlp_date is used in a temporal filter meant to identify when a clinical event occurred (e.g., BETWEEN for cohort entry), flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM note_nlp WHERE note_nlp_concept_id = 201826 AND nlp_date BETWEEN '2023-01-01' AND '2023-12-31'
example_correct: SELECT nn.* FROM note_nlp nn JOIN note n ON nn.note_id = n.note_id WHERE nn.note_nlp_concept_id = 201826 AND n.note_date BETWEEN '2023-01-01' AND '2023-12-31'

name: drug_strength_numerator_denominator_for_concentration
description: drug_strength stores concentration-based drugs using numerator_value/numerator_unit_concept_id and denominator_value/denominator_unit_concept_id (e.g., 500mg/5mL). Queries that only check amount_value miss these liquid/injectable formulations entirely.
logic: If a query selects dosage from drug_strength using only amount_value without also checking numerator_value/denominator_value (via COALESCE or OR), flag as warning — concentration-based drugs use numerator/denominator instead of amount.
example_violation: SELECT ds.amount_value, ds.amount_unit_concept_id FROM drug_strength ds WHERE ds.drug_concept_id = 19078461
example_correct: SELECT COALESCE(ds.amount_value, ds.numerator_value) AS dose_value, COALESCE(ds.amount_unit_concept_id, ds.numerator_unit_concept_id) AS dose_unit, ds.denominator_value, ds.denominator_unit_concept_id FROM drug_strength ds WHERE ds.drug_concept_id = 19078461

name: drug_strength_unit_concept_ids_must_be_unit_domain
description: drug_strength.amount_unit_concept_id, numerator_unit_concept_id, and denominator_unit_concept_id must all reference concepts from the Unit domain (typically UCUM vocabulary). Using Drug or Measurement concepts as units is incorrect.
logic: If any hardcoded concept_id in drug_strength unit columns resolves to domain_id != 'Unit', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_strength WHERE numerator_unit_concept_id = 1125315 -- Drug concept used as unit
example_correct: SELECT * FROM drug_strength WHERE numerator_unit_concept_id = 8576 -- mg (Unit domain)

name: specimen_source_values_not_for_analytical_filtering
description: specimen.anatomic_site_source_value and specimen.disease_status_source_value are free-text source fields. Use anatomic_site_concept_id and disease_status_concept_id (standard concepts) for analytical queries.
logic: If a query filters specimen.anatomic_site_source_value or specimen.disease_status_source_value with string matching for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM specimen WHERE anatomic_site_source_value LIKE '%liver%'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM specimen WHERE anatomic_site_concept_id IN (SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM concept_ancestor WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 4002608)

name: cost_amount_allowed_semantics
description: cost.amount_allowed is the maximum amount the payer will reimburse for the service, distinct from total_charge (billed) and total_paid (actually paid). Do not use amount_allowed as a synonym for total_paid or total_cost.
logic: If a query aliases amount_allowed as 'total_cost', 'total_paid', or 'billed_amount', or sums amount_allowed with total_paid/total_charge, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT SUM(amount_allowed + total_paid) AS total_expense FROM cost
example_correct: SELECT SUM(amount_allowed) AS allowed_amount, SUM(total_paid) AS payment FROM cost

name: cost_paid_ingredient_cost_drug_specific
description: cost.paid_ingredient_cost and cost.paid_dispensing_fee are specific to pharmacy/drug claims. Querying these columns for non-drug cost records (e.g., procedure or visit costs) returns NULL or meaningless values. Filter cost_domain_id = 'Drug' when using these columns.
logic: If a query references cost.paid_ingredient_cost or cost.paid_dispensing_fee without filtering cost_domain_id = 'Drug', flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT SUM(paid_ingredient_cost) FROM cost
example_correct: SELECT SUM(paid_ingredient_cost) FROM cost WHERE cost_domain_id = 'Drug'

name: visit_detail_source_concept_id_not_for_filtering
description: visit_detail.visit_detail_source_concept_id stores the source-system concept for the visit detail type. For analytical queries, use visit_detail_concept_id (standard concept). Source concepts are non-standard and site-specific.
logic: If a query filters visit_detail.visit_detail_source_concept_id for cohort or analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_source_concept_id = 32037
example_correct: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_concept_id = 32037

name: visit_detail_admitted_discharged_source_values_not_for_filtering
description: visit_detail.admitted_from_source_value and visit_detail.discharged_to_source_value are free-text source fields. Use admitted_from_concept_id and discharged_to_concept_id for standardized filtering.
logic: If a query filters visit_detail.admitted_from_source_value or discharged_to_source_value for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE admitted_from_source_value = 'Emergency'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM visit_detail vd JOIN concept c ON vd.admitted_from_concept_id = c.concept_id WHERE c.concept_name = 'Emergency Room Visit'

name: visit_occurrence_admitted_discharged_source_values_not_for_filtering
description: visit_occurrence.admitted_from_source_value and visit_occurrence.discharged_to_source_value are free-text source fields. Use admitted_from_concept_id and discharged_to_concept_id for standardized filtering.
logic: If a query filters visit_occurrence.admitted_from_source_value or discharged_to_source_value for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence WHERE discharged_to_source_value = 'Home'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence WHERE discharged_to_concept_id = 8536

name: visit_occurrence_source_concept_id_not_for_visit_type
description: visit_occurrence.visit_source_concept_id stores the source-system concept for the visit. For identifying visit type (inpatient, outpatient, ER), use visit_concept_id (standard). Source concepts are non-standard.
logic: If a query filters visit_occurrence.visit_source_concept_id to identify visit types for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_source_concept_id = 9201
example_correct: SELECT * FROM visit_occurrence WHERE visit_concept_id = 9201

name: person_source_concept_ids_not_for_demographic_filtering
description: person.gender_source_concept_id, race_source_concept_id, and ethnicity_source_concept_id store source-system concepts for demographics. For analytical queries, use gender_concept_id, race_concept_id, and ethnicity_concept_id (standard concepts).
logic: If a query filters person.gender_source_concept_id, race_source_concept_id, or ethnicity_source_concept_id for cohort identification, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM person WHERE gender_source_concept_id = 8532
example_correct: SELECT * FROM person WHERE gender_concept_id = 8532

name: drug_exposure_dose_unit_source_value_is_free_text
description: drug_exposure.dose_unit_source_value is a free-text string from the source system representing the dosing unit. It is not standardized. For unit information, join to drug_strength and use its unit_concept_id columns.
logic: If a query filters or parses drug_exposure.dose_unit_source_value for analytical dosing calculations, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE dose_unit_source_value = 'mg'
example_correct: SELECT ds.amount_unit_concept_id FROM drug_exposure de JOIN drug_strength ds ON de.drug_concept_id = ds.drug_concept_id WHERE ds.invalid_reason IS NULL

name: drug_exposure_route_source_value_not_for_filtering
description: drug_exposure.route_source_value is a free-text string from the source system. For standardized route filtering, use route_concept_id joined to concept.
logic: If a query filters drug_exposure.route_source_value for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE route_source_value = 'PO'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM drug_exposure WHERE route_concept_id = 4132161 -- Oral route

name: measurement_unit_source_concept_id_not_for_filtering
description: measurement.unit_source_concept_id stores the source-system concept for the unit. For analytical queries, use unit_concept_id (standard UCUM concept). Source unit concepts are non-standard.
logic: If a query filters measurement.unit_source_concept_id for analytical unit comparisons, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE unit_source_concept_id = 8840
example_correct: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE unit_concept_id = 8840

name: measurement_value_source_value_is_free_text
description: measurement.value_source_value is a free-text field storing the raw result string from the source system (e.g., '>10.0', 'Positive', '3+'). It is not standardized. Use value_as_number and value_as_concept_id for analytical queries.
logic: If a query filters or parses measurement.value_source_value for analytical result extraction, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE value_source_value = 'Positive'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM measurement WHERE value_as_concept_id = 45884084 -- Positive concept

name: observation_value_source_value_is_free_text
description: observation.value_source_value is a free-text field storing the raw result from the source. Use value_as_number, value_as_string, or value_as_concept_id for standardized analytical queries.
logic: If a query filters observation.value_source_value for analytical purposes when value_as_number or value_as_concept_id would be appropriate, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE value_source_value = 'Yes'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE value_as_concept_id = 4188539 -- Yes concept

name: observation_qualifier_source_value_not_for_filtering
description: observation.qualifier_source_value is a free-text field for the source qualifier. Use qualifier_concept_id for standardized analytical filtering.
logic: If a query filters observation.qualifier_source_value for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE qualifier_source_value = 'severe'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM observation WHERE qualifier_concept_id = 4023515 -- Severe

name: condition_status_source_value_not_for_filtering
description: condition_occurrence.condition_status_source_value is a free-text string from the source system. Use condition_status_concept_id for standardized diagnostic status filtering.
logic: If a query filters condition_occurrence.condition_status_source_value for analytical purposes, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_status_source_value = 'Final'
example_correct: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_status_concept_id = 32902 -- Final diagnosis

name: death_type_concept_id_domain_constraint
description: death.death_type_concept_id indicates the provenance of the death record (e.g., EHR, death certificate, claims). It must reference Type Concept domain concepts, not clinical domains.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in death.death_type_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Type Concept', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM death WHERE death_type_concept_id = 4329847 -- Condition concept used as death type
example_correct: SELECT * FROM death WHERE death_type_concept_id = 32817 -- EHR (Type Concept domain)

name: cost_type_concept_id_domain_constraint
description: cost.cost_type_concept_id indicates the provenance of the cost record. It must reference Type Concept domain concepts, not clinical or financial amount concepts.
logic: If a hardcoded concept_id in cost.cost_type_concept_id resolves to domain_id != 'Type Concept', flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM cost WHERE cost_type_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept as cost type
example_correct: SELECT * FROM cost WHERE cost_type_concept_id = 32814 -- Claim (Type Concept domain)

name: cross_join_from_comma_separated_tables
description: Listing tables in FROM with commas and no WHERE join condition produces a Cartesian cross join. In OMOP with tables containing millions of rows, this generates billions of rows and crashes queries.
logic: If two or more clinical tables appear in a comma-separated FROM clause without a corresponding WHERE clause equating join keys, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence, drug_exposure WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826
example_correct: SELECT * FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN drug_exposure de ON co.person_id = de.person_id WHERE co.condition_concept_id = 201826

name: union_vs_union_all_for_clinical_events
description: UNION removes duplicates, which is rarely appropriate for clinical event data because two identical-looking rows may represent legitimately separate events (e.g., two ER visits on the same day). Use UNION ALL to preserve all records unless deduplication is explicitly intended.
logic: If UNION (without ALL) is used to combine results from clinical event tables, flag as warning — UNION ALL is almost always more appropriate for event data.
example_violation: SELECT person_id, condition_start_date AS event_date FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826 UNION SELECT person_id, drug_exposure_start_date FROM drug_exposure WHERE drug_concept_id = 1125315
example_correct: SELECT person_id, condition_start_date AS event_date FROM condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id = 201826 UNION ALL SELECT person_id, drug_exposure_start_date FROM drug_exposure WHERE drug_concept_id = 1125315

name: ambiguous_column_reference_in_multi_table_query
description: When multiple OMOP tables are joined, columns like person_id, provider_id, care_site_id, visit_occurrence_id exist in many tables. Referencing these without a table alias is ambiguous and will cause SQL errors or unpredictable behavior.
logic: If a multi-table query references person_id, provider_id, care_site_id, visit_occurrence_id, or visit_detail_id in SELECT, WHERE, GROUP BY, or ORDER BY without a table alias prefix, flag as warning.
example_violation: SELECT person_id, condition_concept_id FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN person p ON co.person_id = p.person_id WHERE person_id = 12345
example_correct: SELECT co.person_id, co.condition_concept_id FROM condition_occurrence co JOIN person p ON co.person_id = p.person_id WHERE co.person_id = 12345

name: visit_detail_type_concept_id_is_provenance
description: visit_detail.visit_detail_type_concept_id represents the provenance of the visit detail record (EHR, claim, etc.), NOT the clinical type of the visit detail. Use visit_detail_concept_id for the clinical visit type (ICU, ward, etc.).
logic: If visit_detail.visit_detail_type_concept_id is filtered with visit-type concepts (e.g., ICU, ward, inpatient) rather than provenance Type Concept domain concepts, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_type_concept_id = 32037 -- ICU concept used as type
example_correct: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE visit_detail_concept_id = 32037 -- ICU as visit detail concept

name: visit_detail_admitted_from_discharged_to_domain
description: visit_detail.admitted_from_concept_id and visit_detail.discharged_to_concept_id must reference concepts from the Visit or Place of Service domain, similar to visit_occurrence. Using clinical concepts is incorrect.
logic: If hardcoded concept_ids in visit_detail.admitted_from_concept_id or discharged_to_concept_id resolve to clinical domains (Condition, Drug, Procedure) instead of Visit/Place of Service, flag as error.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE admitted_from_concept_id = 201826 -- Condition concept as admission source
example_correct: SELECT * FROM visit_detail WHERE admitted_from_concept_id = 8870 -- Emergency Room

name: attribute_definition_is_legacy_table
description: attribute_definition is an optional legacy table in CDM v5.4 with no foreign key relationships to other tables. It has a single column (attribute_definition_id) as PK and no edges. Joining it to clinical or vocabulary tables is incorrect.
logic: If attribute_definition is joined to any other OMOP table, flag as error — it has no FK relationships.
example_violation: SELECT * FROM attribute_definition ad JOIN person p ON ad.attribute_definition_id = p.person_id
example_correct: SELECT * FROM attribute_definition -- Standalone reference table only
